mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5% signals labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending growth and corporate earnings expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 20d returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days during early-to-mid economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 60d returns during expansion phases, with out-of-sample hit rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates directly correlate with stronger corporate revenue fundamentals, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in earnings upgrades.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth shows zero predictive power for 5d forward returns, with Rank IC consistently near zero across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 245.0 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL breaches above 245.0 are followed by positive 20d returns in >55% of cases, invalidating the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales growth signals weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during late-cycle economic phases.
failure_condition: Demand exhaustion signals produce positive 20d returns in >60% of late-cycle observations, contradicting the earnings downgrade mechanism.
